title: The Nature of Variables - Scenario-Based Quiz

questions.question: In Scenario 1 (BioGrow experiment), what is the INDEPENDENT variable?
options: The growth rate of the plants
The concentration of the BioGrow solution
The number of plants in each greenhouse
The type of greenhouse used

questions.question: In Scenario 1 (BioGrow experiment), what is the DEPENDENT variable?
options: The concentration of the BioGrow solution
The volume of water given daily
The growth rate (or height) of the plants
The initial height of the plants

questions.question: In Scenario 1 (BioGrow experiment), which of the following is an EXTRANEOUS variable?
options: The concentration of BioGrow solution
The growth rate of the plants
Ambient temperature and humidity in the greenhouses
The volume of solution given daily

questions.question: In Scenario 2 (water hyacinth bioethanol experiment), what is the INDEPENDENT variable?
options: The volume of bioethanol yielded
The amount of yeast (Saccharomyces cerevisiae)
The fermentation temperature
The pH level of the mash

questions.question: In Scenario 2 (water hyacinth bioethanol experiment), what is the DEPENDENT variable?
options: The amount of yeast used
The fermentation temperature
The volume of bioethanol yielded
The pH level of the mash

questions.question: In Scenario 2 (water hyacinth bioethanol experiment), which of the following is an EXTRANEOUS variable?
options: The amount of yeast (Saccharomyces cerevisiae)
The volume of bioethanol yielded
The fermentation temperature and pH level (controlled)
The initial moisture content of the water hyacinth pulp

questions.question: In Scenario 3 (phone charger experiment), what is the INDEPENDENT variable?
options: The charging time of the phone
The charger and charging cable combination
The phone brand
The battery capacity

questions.question: In Scenario 3 (phone charger experiment), what is the DEPENDENT variable?
options: The charger and charging cable combination
The phone brand
The charging time of the phone
The number of chargers tested

questions.question: In Scenario 3 (phone charger experiment), which of the following is an EXTRANEOUS variable?
options: The charger and charging cable combination
The charging time
Applications currently used on the phone
The phone's screen size

questions.question: In Scenario 4 (solar panel experiment), what is the INDEPENDENT variable?
options: The energy output (kWh) of the solar panels
The exposure to urban dust and air pollution (Array A vs. Array B environments)
The panel tilt angle
The type of solar panel used

questions.question: In Scenario 4 (solar panel experiment), what is the DEPENDENT variable?
options: The exposure to urban dust and air pollution
The panel tilt angle
The energy output (kWh) of the solar panels
The solar irradiance

questions.question: In Scenario 4 (solar panel experiment), which of the following is an EXTRANEOUS variable?
options: Array A vs. Array B environments
Energy output (kWh)
Panel tilt angle (controlled)
The color of the solar panels

questions.question: In Scenario 5 (TEG experiment), what is the INDEPENDENT variable?
options: The voltage produced by the TEG
The temperature difference (ΔT) between the heat source and ambient air
The electrical conductivity of the mud
The type of mud used

questions.question: In Scenario 5 (TEG experiment), what is the DEPENDENT variable?
options: The temperature difference (ΔT)
The electrical conductivity of the mud
The voltage produced by the TEG
The initial moisture content of the mud

questions.question: In Scenario 5 (TEG experiment), which of the following is an EXTRANEOUS variable?
options: The temperature difference (ΔT)
The voltage produced
The electrical conductivity of the mud
The heat source used

questions.question: In Scenario 2 (water hyacinth bioethanol experiment), what was held completely constant by the researchers?
options: The amount of yeast
The fermentation temperature and pH level
The volume of bioethanol
The type of water hyacinth

questions.question: In Scenario 5 (TEG experiment), what does TEG stand for?
options: Thermal Energy Generator
Thermo-Electric Generator
Total Energy Grid
Thermal Electric Grid
